payload.description: CHAR_B: "This place is giving haunted cafeteria."

cast.voice_hint: Main character energy, minus the delusion. Actually does the thing instead of posting about doing the thing. Shows up when it counts, deflects the credit, moves on.
Main character energy, minus the delusion. Actually does the thing instead of posting about doing the thing. Shows up when it counts, deflects the credit, moves on.
Knows a guy. Shows up and things start happening — a collab lands, a door opens, a party forms. Doesn't do the work, just makes the work possible by being in the room.

location.notes: decayed, haunted by past; abandoned building, old temple, wreckage

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: This place is giving haunted cafeteria.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: It's fine.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: You keep saying that.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: And it keeps being true. Come on.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: See that. Don't touch it.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Why.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Because the last person who touched it is not here anymore.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Noted. Noted and logged.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Your footwork. You're telegraphing.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: I'm literally just walking.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: In a ruin. That is never just walking.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: ...Okay. Okay, yeah.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Okay so — don't panic — but I found something.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Where did you even come from.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: The far wing. There's a whole situation in there. Also hi.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: Is the situation good or bad.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: Depends on what you're trying to do.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: There's a way through. But someone has to go first.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: I'll go.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Hold on—

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: I've got it. I've actually got it this time.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: That worked.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: You knew it was going to work.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: I had a guess. You made it a fact.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: I feel like I made that happen.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: You showed up and waved something around.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: That is exactly what making things happen looks like.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: ...Honestly. Not wrong.

lines.speaker: CHAR_B
lines.text: So. Same time next week?

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Same time. Different ruin.

lines.speaker: CHAR_C
lines.text: I know a place. Actually, I know three places.

lines.speaker: CHAR_A
lines.text: Of course you do.

state.conditioning_notes: `<CHAR_A>`'s fear of letting the group down shapes the instructor role — they call out the telegraphing footwork not to criticize but because they need `<CHAR_B>` to be ready. `<CHAR_B>`'s identical fear drives the opposite behavior: a hunger to prove competence by going first, not waiting to be told they're ready. The mentor_student relationship means `<CHAR_A>` already knows what `<CHAR_B>` can do; the beat lands when `<CHAR_A>` says "you made it a fact" — credit passed, no ceremony. `<CHAR_C>` doesn't solve the problem; they create the condition in which the problem becomes solvable, and then immediately takes implicit credit. The ruin location shapes everything at the behavioral level: characters don't relax here, they stay half-alert even when they're joking — the "just walking" exchange exists because in a ruin, walking is a skill. The low tension ceiling (1–3) means all of this plays out as a quiet, competent afternoon, not a crisis — the stakes are about trust and growth, not survival. The arc peaks at beat 4–5 when `<CHAR_B>` volunteers to go first and comes back down into warmth and the low-stakes comedy of `<CHAR_C>`'s self-congratulation.
